requirements.summary: Integrate Internet Identity authentication with login/logout UI in the sidebar, displaying user principal when authenticated and restricting access appropriately
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Login with Internet Identity' button is visible when the user is not authenticated.
Clicking the login button opens the Internet Identity authentication flow.
After successful login, the user's authenticated state is reflected in the UI (e.g., principal shown or welcome message).
A 'Logout' button is available when the user is authenticated.
Clicking logout clears the session and returns the user to the unauthenticated state.
The useActor hook uses the authenticated identity so backend calls are made on behalf of the logged-in principal.
The existing useInternetIdentity hook and useActor hook are used without modification.

file_operations.description: Add authentication UI to the sidebar by importing and using the useInternetIdentity hook. When the user is not authenticated, display a 'Login with Internet Identity' button in the sidebar footer or header area. When authenticated, show a user indicator (truncated principal or welcome message) and a 'Logout' button. Wire the login button to call the login function from useInternetIdentity, and the logout button to call the logout function. Ensure the UI reflects the isAuthenticated state from the hook.
